content:
```json
[
	{"bbox_2d": [814, 67, 926, 81], "text_content": "STUSB4500"},
	{"bbox_2d": [756, 83, 926, 95], "text_content": "Description of the features"},
	{"bbox_2d": [73, 122, 88, 139], "text_content": "3"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 122, 463, 140], "text_content": "Description of the features"},
	{"bbox_2d": [73, 177, 99, 191], "text_content": "3.1"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 177, 285, 193], "text_content": "CC interface"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 197, 925, 226], "text_content": "The STUSB4500 controls the connection to the configuration channel (CC) pins, CC1 and CC2, through two main blocks: the CC line interface block and the CC control logic block."},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 226, 419, 239], "text_content": "The CC line interface block is used to:"},
	{"bbox_2d": [211, 242, 528, 255], "text_content": "Set pull-down termination mode on the CC pins"},
	{"bbox_2d": [211, 258, 749, 271], "text_content": "Monitor the CC pin voltage values related to the attachment detection thresholds"},
	{"bbox_2d": [211, 274, 475, 287], "text_content": "Protect the CC pins against overvoltage"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 290, 403, 303], "text_content": "The CC control logic block is used to:"},
	{"bbox_2d": [211, 309, 782, 322], "text_content": "Execute the Type-C FSM related to the sink power role with debug accessory support"},
	{"bbox_2d": [211, 325, 672, 338], "text_content": "Determine the electrical state for each CC pin related to the detected thresholds"},
	{"bbox_2d": [211, 341, 926, 360], "text_content": "Evaluate the conditions relative to the CC pin states and the VBUS voltage value to transition from one state to another in the Type-C FSM"},
	{"bbox_2d": [211, 371, 497, 384], "text_content": "Advertise a valid source-to-sink connection"},
	{"bbox_2d": [211, 387, 604, 400], "text_content": "Determine the attached device mode: source or debug accessory"},
	{"bbox_2d": [211, 403, 672, 416], "text_content": "Determine cable orientation to allow external routing of the USB data"},
	{"bbox_2d": [211, 419, 821, 432], "text_content": "Manage USB Type-C power capability on VBUS: USB default, medium or high current mode"},
	{"bbox_2d": [211, 435, 366, 448], "text_content": "Handle hardware faults"},
	{"bbox_2d": [73, 467, 99, 481], "text_content": "3.2"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 467, 377, 483], "text_content": "Power delivery blocks"},
	{"bbox_2d": [73, 501, 109, 514], "text_content": "3.2.1"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 501, 285, 517], "text_content": "Physical layer"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 519, 926, 579], "text_content": "The physical layer defines the signaling technology for USB power delivery. It is the physical link between CC pins and protocol layer. In Tx mode, it receives packet data from the protocol layer, calculates and appends a CRC, encodes the payload (i.e. packet data and CRC) and transmits the packet (i.e. preamble, SOP, payload, CRC and EOP) using biphase mark coding (i.e. BMC) over CC pins. In Rx mode, it recovers the clock and the data, detects the SOP, decodes the received data including the CRC, detects the EOP and validates the CRC."},
	{"bbox_2d": [73, 595, 109, 608], "text_content": "3.2.2"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 595, 278, 611], "text_content": "Protocol layer"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 614, 887, 643], "text_content": "The protocol layer has the responsibility to manage the messages from/to the physical layer. It automatically manages the protocol receive timeouts, the message counter, the retry counter and the GoodCRC messages. It communicates with the internal policy engine."},
	{"bbox_2d": [73, 679, 109, 692], "text_content": "3.2.3"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 679, 273, 695], "text_content": "Policy engine"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 692, 877, 705], "text_content": "The policy engine implements the power negotiation with the connected device according to its sink role. It implements all state machines controlling the protocol layer that forms and schedules the messages."},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 718, 624, 731], "text_content": "The policy engine uses the protocol layer to send/receive messages."},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 734, 923, 763], "text_content": "The policy engine interprets the device policy manager's input in order to implement policy for port and directs the protocol layer to send appropriate messages."},
	{"bbox_2d": [73, 780, 109, 793], "text_content": "3.2.4"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 780, 348, 796], "text_content": "Device policy manager"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 793, 866, 822], "text_content": "The device policy manager deals with the power capability request and change management. It operates according to the decision algorithm described in the following section."},
	{"bbox_2d": [73, 838, 99, 852], "text_content": "3.3"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 838, 310, 854], "text_content": "Auto-run mode"},
	{"bbox_2d": [166, 857, 925, 895], "text_content": "The STUSB4500 implements a hardcoded decision algorithm that allows the device to negotiate in autonomous way a power delivery transaction with a source according to the PDO (power data objects) profiles programmed in the NVM."},
	{"bbox_2d": [73, 975, 159, 984], "text_content": "DS12499 - Rev 8"},
	{"bbox_2d": [868, 975, 924, 984], "text_content": "page 10/42"}
]
```
